questions.explanation: RTP is the long-run expected payout percentage of total wagers returned to players, not a per-spin guarantee.

options.A: The percentage of total wagers the game is expected to pay back to players over the long run
options.B: The maximum possible win on a single spin
options.C: The probability that a player wins any prize on a spin
options.D: The time it takes for the reels to stop after pressing Spin

questions.question: In a slot game, what does RTP (Return to Player) represent?

questions.explanation: Option B is unambiguous, measurable, and directly implementable (trigger condition + outcome).

options.A: Make the bonus round exciting and generous
options.B: If 3 Scatter symbols land anywhere, award 10 free spins
options.D: Increase wins when the player is losing

questions.question: Which statement is the clearest, testable game rule suitable for implementation?

questions.explanation: Update() runs once per frame; FixedUpdate() is tied to the physics timestep, and Awake/OnEnable are lifecycle events.
questions.question: In Unity, which built-in method is called once per rendered frame (commonly used for non-physics gameplay updates)?

questions.explanation: git revert adds a new commit that undoes a prior commit, preserving shared history—important for team workflows.
questions.question: In Git, which command creates a new commit that reverses the changes introduced by a previous commit (without rewriting history)?

questions.explanation: Prefix sums + binary search gives O(log n) per sample after O(n) preprocessing, which is efficient for high-frequency draws.

options.A: Build a prefix-sum (cumulative weights) array once and use binary search per sample
options.B: Sort prizes alphabetically and pick the first one
options.C: For each sample, linearly scan all weights and stop when a threshold is reached
options.D: Use a stack and pop the top prize each time

questions.question: You need to select a weighted random prize from 10,000 entries many times per second. Which approach is typically most efficient for repeated sampling?

questions.explanation: ECS places behavior in systems operating over data-only components, improving modularity, testability, and performance.

options.A: Inside a PaylineEvaluationSystem that processes entities with relevant components (e.g., Reels, Paytable, Bet)
options.B: Inside the Entity class as methods like Entity.CalculateWins()
options.C: Inside a single global singleton object that stores all game state and logic
options.D: Inside the rendering shader so it runs on the GPU

questions.question: In an ECS (Entity Component System) architecture, where should the logic that evaluates paylines and awards wins typically live?

questions.explanation: Profiling is the correct first step to pinpoint CPU/GPU time, GC allocations, and call stacks causing spikes.

options.A: Use the Unity Profiler to capture a timeline and locate the expensive functions/allocations
options.B: Immediately rewrite the bonus round in C++
options.C: Disable VSync and ship if FPS increases
options.D: Randomly remove code until the spikes disappear

questions.question: A Unity slot prototype shows occasional frame spikes when entering the bonus round. What is the best first step to identify the cause?

questions.explanation: Passing snapshots/messages to the main thread prevents concurrent mutation and respects engine thread-affinity (e.g., Unity UI must be updated on main thread).

options.A: Have the worker thread write results into an immutable snapshot (or thread-safe queue) and apply it on the main thread
options.B: Let both threads read/write the same game-state objects without locks for maximum speed
options.C: Call Unity UI APIs directly from the worker thread
options.D: Use a busy-wait loop on the main thread until the worker finishes

questions.question: You run reel simulation and win evaluation on a worker thread to keep the UI responsive. Which design best avoids data races when updating the UI with results?

questions.explanation: Quaternions avoid gimbal lock and slerp provides smooth rotational interpolation along the shortest path.

options.B: Euler angles with linear interpolation on each axis
options.C: A 2D vector and dot product only
options.D: A 4x4 matrix with element-wise linear interpolation

questions.question: A 3D bonus wheel must rotate smoothly from its current orientation to a target orientation without gimbal lock. Which representation/interpolation is most appropriate?

questions.explanation: Server-side validation with cryptographic signing prevents client tampering and makes codes unforgeable without the secret key.

options.A: Validate codes on a server and sign/verify them using a secret key (e.g., HMAC), granting rewards only after server approval
options.B: Store all valid codes in a client-side JSON file and check locally
options.C: Obfuscate the code-checking function in the client and rely on that for security
options.D: Generate codes by hashing the current time on the client and accept any matching format

questions.question: You are asked to add 'publicity code' support: players enter a code that grants a bonus, but the code must not be guessable or forgeable by reading the client. Which approach is most secure?
